proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 32 candidate features, at least one binary feature is strongly and positively associated with objective_response and behaves like an active treatment indicator (i.e., higher response rate when present).
feature_008 = 1 is associated with a higher objective_response rate than feature_008 = 0 (positive main effect).
feature_013 = 1 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than feature_013 = 0 (negative prognostic feature).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 15.9% when feature_008=0 (n=29,924) vs 36.1% when feature_008=1 (n=20,076); absolute difference +20.1 pp; chi-square p < 1e-300. feature_008 is by far the largest positive marginal predictor and is treated as the candidate treatment variable for the rest of the analysis.
Response rate 34.6% when feature_013=0 vs 15.4% when feature_013=1; absolute difference -19.2 pp; chi-square p < 1e-300. feature_013 is the strongest negative marginal predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 = 1 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than feature_015 = 0.
feature_021 = 1 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than feature_021 = 0.
feature_027 = 1 is associated with a lower objective_response rate than feature_027 = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response 26.0% (feature_015=0) vs 16.0% (feature_015=1); diff -10.0 pp; chi-square p ~1.9e-98.
Response 25.0% (feature_021=0) vs 15.0% (feature_021=1); diff -10.1 pp; chi-square p ~5.4e-56.
Response 24.2% (feature_027=0) vs 17.6% (feature_027=1); diff -6.6 pp; chi-square p ~3.0e-9.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_022 (PSA-like, heavy-tailed continuous, range 0.12 to 3622) is associated with lower probability of objective_response.
feature_001 has a negative ordinal relationship with objective_response: higher levels (0 < 1 < 2) are associated with lower response.

analyses.result_summary: Point-biserial r = -0.087 (p ~2.5e-85). Above-median feature_022 has 18.2% response vs 29.8% at-or-below median. Direction: higher feature_022 -> lower response, consistent with PSA biology.
Response by feature_001 level: 0=28.2%, 1=22.8%, 2=18.2%. Monotonically decreasing; chi-square p ~5.4e-72; ordinal slope (logit per level) -0.33 in multivariable model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_002 (continuous, median 3.8, range 1.5 to 5.5; albumin-like) is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher feature_020 (count-like, median 3.0, range 0 to 24.6) is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_024 (heavy-skew continuous, median 3.34) is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Point-biserial r = +0.0205 (p ~4.4e-6). Higher feature_002 quartile has progressively higher response (23.1% -> 25.1% across quartiles). Small but consistent positive prognostic effect.
Point-biserial r = -0.050 (p ~2.5e-29). Patients with feature_020 > 0 have 23.2% response vs 26.3% when feature_020 = 0. Negative prognostic.
Point-biserial r = -0.024 (p ~5.2e-8). Small but significant negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment for all candidate features in a single logistic regression, feature_008 retains a strong positive coefficient on log-odds of objective_response (independent treatment effect).

The other balanced binary features (feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_023) have NO independent main effect on objective_response in the multivariable model.

analyses.result_summary: In a logistic regression with all 31 non-constant features simultaneously, feature_008 coefficient = +1.20 (SE 0.023, z=52.6, p<1e-300). Other significant predictors: feature_013 (-1.13), feature_021 (-0.74), feature_015 (-0.69), feature_027 (-0.43), feature_001 (-0.33 per level), feature_022 (-0.001 per unit), feature_020 (-0.036), feature_002 (+0.13), feature_024 (-0.007).

analyses.code: Same multivariable Logit; inspect coefficients/p-values for feature_005, 006, 011, 017, 019, 004, 023
analyses.result_summary: Coefficients (p-values): feature_005 -0.029 (0.20), feature_006 +0.002 (0.93), feature_011 +0.002 (0.95), feature_017 +0.026 (0.49), feature_019 +0.029 (0.35), feature_004 +0.014 (0.79), feature_023 -0.025 (0.31). None are significant adjusted; consistent with no independent prognostic role.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 on objective_response is heterogeneous: it is significantly attenuated when feature_013 = 1 (formal treatment-by-feature_013 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Logistic with main effects + interaction: beta(feature_008) = +2.08, beta(feature_013) = -0.12, beta(feature_008 x feature_013) = -1.98 (p < 1e-300). Sub-table: among feature_013=0, response goes from 16.9% (untreated) to 61.0% (treated, +44 pp). Among feature_013=1, response goes from 15.0% to 15.8% (no benefit).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 is also abolished when feature_015 = 1 (treatment-by-feature_015 interaction is large and negative).

analyses.result_summary: beta(interaction feature_008 x feature_015) = -1.24 (p ~1.4e-93). Stratified rates: feature_015=0: 16.0%(off) -> 41.1%(on), +25 pp. feature_015=1: 15.7%(off) -> 16.5%(on), no benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 is also abolished when feature_021 = 1 (treatment-by-feature_021 interaction is large and negative).
The treatment effect of feature_008 is also abolished when feature_027 = 1 (treatment-by-feature_027 interaction is large and negative).

analyses.result_summary: beta(interaction) = -1.14 (p ~3.3e-42). feature_021=0: 16.0%(off) -> 38.5%(on); feature_021=1: 14.7%(off) -> 15.3%(on), no benefit.
beta(interaction) = -1.28 (p ~1.8e-19). feature_027=0: response goes 16.0%->37.0% with treatment. feature_027=1: 18.4%(off) -> 16.2%(on), no benefit (slightly worse).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_022 (PSA-like) attenuates the treatment effect of feature_008: the feature_008 x log(feature_022+1) interaction is negative and significant.

analyses.code: log_psa = log1p(feature_022); Logit on feature_008 + log_psa + interaction
analyses.result_summary: beta(feature_008) = +2.39, beta(log_psa) = -0.087, beta(interaction) = -0.46 (p ~5e-128, z=-24.1). Quartile cross-table: in PSA Q1, treatment lifts response 17.2%->53.9% (+36.7); in Q4, only 13.9%->17.3% (+3.4). Treatment benefit decays as PSA rises.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Increasing feature_001 level slightly attenuates the treatment effect of feature_008 (treatment-by-feature_001 interaction is negative on the log-odds scale).

analyses.result_summary: Univariable interaction coefficient is small and slightly POSITIVE (+0.14, p ~1.6e-5) when only those two terms are present (because feature_001 is correlated with the suppressors), but in the joint model with all suppressor interactions feature_001 enters mainly as a small additive negative (-0.36/level). Subgroup table within the 'good' subgroup (no suppressors) shows treatment lifts response from 21%(stage 0) -> 86%, 16%->78%, 12%->71% across feature_001 = 0/1/2: small attenuation but treatment benefit is large at every stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of feature_008 is concentrated in the subgroup with feature_013 = 0 AND feature_015 = 0 AND feature_021 = 0 AND feature_027 = 0; in patients with any of these set to 1, the treatment confers no additional benefit.

analyses.code: good = (feature_013==0)&(feature_015==0)&(feature_021==0)&(feature_027==0); bad = ~good; compare feature_008 effect in each
analyses.result_summary: GOOD subgroup (n=15,681; 31.4% of cohort): response 17.2%(off, n=9,356) vs 79.8%(on, n=6,325); absolute benefit +62.6 pp; chi-square p ~0. BAD subgroup (n=34,319): response 15.3%(off) vs 16.1%(on); absolute benefit +0.8 pp, not clinically meaningful. Stratified by individual suppressor, treatment benefit within each 'bad' stratum is also <2 pp and statistically null (feature_013=1 p=0.09; 015=1 p=0.27; 021=1 p=0.57; 027=1 p=0.30).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a single logistic model that includes all four interaction terms (feature_008 x each of feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) plus the strongest covariates, every interaction remains independently negative and large.

analyses.code: Logit y on feature_008, four binaries, four interactions, feature_022, feature_001, feature_020, feature_002, feature_024
analyses.result_summary: Joint interactions in a single model: feature_008 +2.62; i(feature_008 x feature_013) -2.23 (p<1e-300); i(x feature_015) -1.64 (p~1e-139); i(x feature_021) -1.56 (p~5e-67); i(x feature_027) -1.66 (p~2e-27). All four suppressors operate near-independently (pairwise correlations ~0). The four-way 'good' subgroup definition is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 'good' subgroup (all four suppressors = 0), no continuous lab feature meaningfully modifies the feature_008 treatment effect (i.e., the four-way binary subgroup is sufficient).

analyses.code: For each continuous feature f: in good subgroup, fit Logit(objective_response ~ feature_008 + f + feature_008:f); record p of interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within the good subgroup the strongest residual interactions are feature_005 (-0.25, p~4e-3) and feature_017 (-0.28, p~3e-2); both small and only nominally significant in a screen of >25 features. Continuous lab interactions including feature_022 (PSA, p=0.79), feature_020 (p=0.97), feature_024 (p=0.61), feature_002 (p=0.66) are non-significant. The four-binary subgroup definition captures essentially all of the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding a low-feature_022 (PSA-like) cut on top of the four-binary good subgroup does NOT further increase the treatment-effect magnitude; treatment benefit is uniformly large across PSA strata within the good subgroup.

analyses.code: for psa_thr in [Q1,Q2,Q3]: subset good & feature_022<thr; compute treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Good + feature_022<6.5 (n=6,988): 18.2%->81.4% (+63.3); good + feature_022<15.9: 17.9%->80.9% (+63.0); good + feature_022<41.3: 17.3%->80.1% (+62.8). The unconditional good subgroup already gives +62.6 pp. PSA does not add information once the 4 suppressors are excluded.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the 'bad' subgroup (any suppressor positive), treatment benefit cannot be rescued by restricting to low PSA (feature_022) — the suppressor interaction dominates.

analyses.code: subset bad & feature_022<thr; compute response by feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Bad subgroup with feature_022<3.10 (n=1,996): 16.8% (off) -> 17.7% (on), diff +0.9 pp. Bad subgroup with feature_022<6.46 (n=5,494): 16.0% -> 17.6%, diff +1.6 pp. Treatment effect cannot be recovered by selecting low-PSA patients within the bad subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_010 (5-level ordinal, range 6-10, plausibly Gleason grade) has no main association with objective_response and no interaction with feature_008.

analyses.result_summary: Response by feature_010: 6=24.4%, 7=23.7%, 8=24.2%, 9=24.2%, 10=24.2%. Chi-square p=0.77 (no main effect). Interaction with feature_008: beta=+0.011, p=0.59. Despite biological plausibility (looks like Gleason), this feature does not predict response or modify treatment in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 (continuous, range 30-90, plausibly age) does not predict objective_response and does not modify the feature_008 effect.

analyses.result_summary: Marginal r = +0.001 (p=0.82), no association. Interaction beta with feature_008 = -0.0002 (p=0.92). 'Age' is not prognostic or predictive here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (likely sodium), feature_014 (likely potassium), feature_032 (likely calcium), feature_026 (likely hemoglobin), feature_018 (likely ALP), feature_009 (likely AST), feature_003 (likely ALT), feature_012 (likely bilirubin), feature_025 (likely creatinine), feature_028 (likely BMI/integer count), feature_029, feature_031 are NOT associated with objective_response and do not modify treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: All marginal p > 0.07 except feature_029 (p=0.22) and feature_014 (p=0.13); none reach 0.05. Interactions with feature_008 are all non-significant (smallest p was feature_002 at p=0.058). These laboratory-like features are not informative here.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A simple decision tree (max_depth=5) of objective_response on the candidate features will rediscover the same subgroup hierarchy: split first on feature_008, then on feature_013, then feature_015, then feature_021, with the high-response leaf being feature_008=1 and all suppressors=0.

analyses.result_summary: Tree splits exactly as predicted: root=feature_008, then within feature_008==1 branches on feature_013 (0/1), then feature_015 (0/1), then feature_021 (0/1). The unique class=1 leaf (predicted response) is feature_008==1 AND feature_013==0 AND feature_015==0 AND feature_021==0. Confirms subgroup structure independently.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The four suppressor variables (feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027) are mutually independent (essentially zero pairwise correlation), so each contributes a separate, near-multiplicative pathway by which treatment effect is lost.

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise correlations (phi): all |r| < 0.005. The four suppressors are statistically independent, so the prevalences combine multiplicatively: P(all 0) = (1-0.55)(1-0.20)(1-0.10)(1-0.03) ~= 0.314, matching the observed good-subgroup share (31.4%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the good subgroup, increasing feature_001 stage modestly reduces the absolute treatment benefit but treatment is still strongly beneficial at every stage (no qualitative interaction within the good subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Within good subgroup: feature_001=0: 21.3% (off) -> 85.9% (on), +64.6 pp (n=5,511). feature_001=1: 15.9% -> 78.1%, +62.2 pp (n=7,824). feature_001=2: 11.9% -> 70.9%, +59.0 pp (n=2,346). Magnitude of treatment benefit is large and significant at every stage; small attenuation (~5 pp) per extra stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The other balanced binaries (feature_005, feature_006, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_023) do NOT have meaningful interactions with feature_008 in the full cohort; only the four suppressors (013, 015, 021, 027) act as effect modifiers.

analyses.code: Interaction screen feature_008 x each binary; rank by p-value
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values (cohort-wide): feature_011 p=0.023 (beta -0.11, small), feature_017 p=0.118, feature_005 p=0.40, feature_004 p=0.95, feature_006 p=0.86, feature_019 p>0.99, feature_023 p=0.89. Only the four suppressors yield interaction p < 1e-19; all other binary-by-treatment interactions are at most weak nuisance signals.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A logistic-regression model that uses only feature_008, the four suppressors, the four corresponding interaction terms, plus feature_022, feature_001, feature_020, feature_002, feature_024 captures the predictive structure for objective_response (AUC > 0.70).

analyses.code: LogisticRegression on the above; AUC = roc_auc_score(y, model.predict_proba)
analyses.result_summary: AUC = 0.7328 on the full 50,000-patient cohort. The interaction-rich joint model substantially outperforms a main-effects-only model (where feature_008 alone gives only ~0.59). The four-way subgroup definition is the dominant signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 has no main effect or treatment-modifying effect, despite being highly prevalent (60%) — it behaves like noise in this cohort.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_005')['objective_response'].mean(); interaction with feature_008; subgroup feature_005=1 vs 0 treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Marginal: feature_005=0 -> 24.3%, feature_005=1 -> 23.9% (chi-square p=0.31). Within feature_008==1, treatment-vs-control diff is identical regardless of feature_005 (-0.001). feature_005 is uninformative for response or treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: feature_008 (the active treatment) increases the probability of objective_response if and only if the patient has feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_027=0; in this subgroup (~31% of cohort) the absolute response increase is approximately +62.6 percentage points (17.2% off-treatment vs 79.8% on-treatment), whereas in the complementary subgroup the treatment effect is null (<+1 pp).

analyses.code: good = (feature_013==0)&(feature_015==0)&(feature_021==0)&(feature_027==0); bad = ~good; compute response rate by feature_008 in each, plus joint model with 4 interactions
analyses.result_summary: Final confirmation: GOOD (n=15,681): off 17.2% (n=9,356), on 79.8% (n=6,325), Delta=+62.6 pp (chi-square p ~0). BAD (n=34,319): off 15.3%, on 16.1%, Delta=+0.8 pp (n.s.). Joint model interaction terms: i(8x13) -2.23, i(8x15) -1.64, i(8x21) -1.56, i(8x27) -1.66, all p<1e-26; main feature_008 effect within good subgroup is +2.62 in log-odds. Direction (+), magnitude (+62.6 pp), and full subgroup definition (4 binaries all 0) all confirmed.
